cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is asking what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) was concerned about when the jade ring design was proposed. The options are A through D. First, I need to look at the relevant part of the passage. The paragraph that mentions the IOC says: "Experts from the International Olympic Committee(IOC)were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. The IOC required that jade rings should be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test. The Shanghai Mint raised that standard to 3-meters. Medals can survive a 3-meter drop test with only minor cracks on the jade rings." So the key words here are "risk of fragility." The concern was that the jade rings might be too fragile and break easily. The IOC wanted them to withstand a 2-meter drop, which implies they were worried about the jade breaking. Looking at the options, option D says "jade is fragile and easy to break up," which matches the "risk of fragility" mentioned.
